articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Cole Palmer scores first England goal in easy Bosnia win
article.description: England kicked off their European Championship preparation with a 3-0 victory over non-qualifiers Bosnia and Herzegovina on Monday at St James' Park, thanks to goals from Cole Palmer, Trent Alexander-Arnold and captain Harry Kane.
article.linkText: Chelsea's Palmer scores 1st England goal in win

images.name: Laurens slams Southgate's decision to cut players for Euro squad
images.caption: Julien Laurens says Gareth Southgate's decision to select an initial 33-player England squad was a mistake after James Maddison was the first player to be axed ahead of Euro 2024.

article.story:
England kicked off their European Championship preparation with a 3-0 victory over non-qualifiers Bosnia and Herzegovina on Monday at St James' Park, thanks to goals from Cole Palmer, Trent Alexander-Arnold and captain Harry Kane.

England stepped up their pace after a dull first half and Palmer, who scored nine penalties for Chelsea this season, broke the deadlock with his first England goal in the 60th minute from the spot after Ezri Konsa was tugged down in the box.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

"It's a great feeling to get my first England goal," Palmer told Channel 4, who was interviewed alongside Kane, a late-game substitute.

"Of course [Kane] would have taken it if he had been on the pitch," a grinning Palmer added.

Liverpool's Alexander-Arnold, who was one of the bright spots on the night, doubled their lead in the 85th minute with his third goal for England when he fired home from a tight angle off a beautiful pass from Manchester City's Jack Grealish.

Bayern Munich striker Kane, who has 12 goals in his last 12 international appearances, scored perhaps one of the easiest goals of his career four minutes later when he poked the ball in from close range after Jarrod Bowen's shot hit Konsa in a scramble in front of goal.

"I'm feeling good," Kane said, after not having played for Bayern since May 8 due to an ongoing back issue. "A lot of the end of season was precaution. It was a good chance to get a good break at the end of the season. I feel fine.

"It's about getting minutes. I'm not someone who takes too long to get sharp."

Several key players including midfielder Jude Bellingham, who won the Champions League with Real Madrid on Saturday, were missing, but the match was much about manager Gareth Southgate deciding on which seven players he must cut before Friday's deadline for their 26-man squad.

He liked what he saw for much of Monday's match.

"We had players that needed minutes and we needed a competitive game," Southgate said. "The opposition was dogged so we had to be patient and keep probing - we felt our quality would tell.

"There was some good individual performances and hopefully we have come through with no issues."

On Palmer, Southgate said: "Look, he showed moments of what he's about. It was of course his first start for us. I think there's more to come as he feels more comfortable in the group and we get to feed him the ball as he needs it.

"He can obviously play in a couple of different positions so he's had a fabulous season and there's no reason he can't have a big impact with us."

The second half salvaged what looked destined to be a disappointing night for the sold-out crowd at St James' Park, in England's first game away from Wembley in a year.

With a talent-laden squad and after their runners-up finish at the last Euros, the Three Lions carry high expectations to Germany.

Southgate now faces a tough task in selecting his squad, although several players including Alexander-Arnold, Eberechi Eze, Conor Gallagher and Palmer stood out on Monday.

The manager's job is made more difficult by the fact Harry Maguire, Luke Shaw and Anthony Gordon are recovering from injuries and were not available on Monday, and Southgate chose to rest Bukayo Saka and John Stones.

Six of England's starting 11 arrived at Monday's match with less than 10 caps.

England host Iceland in their final friendly on Friday at Wembley before departing for Germany.

They face Serbia in their opening Group C match on June 16 in Gelsenkirchen.
